summary: A functional test failed when pip attempted to download a package from GitHub and received repeated 502 responses, causing a network-related test failure.
root_cause: The test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip could not download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip due to repeated 502 responses from github.com, causing an OSError and non-zero exit code.

evidence.reason: Shows the failing test was rerun (indicates a flaky/network-involving test).
Identifies the specific test that failed.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: The pip command failed with an OSError caused by repeated 502 responses from GitHub while attempting to download the test package.
The test harness recorded a non-zero exit code from the pip install command, causing the test to fail.
Summary confirming a single failing test among many passing tests, consistent with an isolated/flaky/network issue.

suggested_fix: Rerun the failing job (the failure is caused by transient 502 responses from github.com). If failures recur, avoid depending on GitHub for this test by mocking the network call or hosting the test package on a reliable local test server / fixture so tests do not fail due to external HTTP 502 errors.
